persona.the_one_thing: Don't walk in asking 'how do we prompt better' — Hannah teaches that clarity of thinking beats clever prompting ('the model isn't confused, it's waiting for you to be clear'), so arrive with ONE precisely-scoped use case (your Slack-delivered country-strategy agents) and she'll engage on craft, not basics. (high confidence; from her own repeated public framing, s3/s8.)
persona.snapshot: Hannah Moran is on Anthropic's Applied AI team (since March 2025) and is one of the public faces of Anthropic's prompting and deployment education — co-presenter of Prompting 101 / Code w/ Claude and contributor to Anthropic's context-engineering-for-agents guide. Her background is consulting, not ML research: Georgetown B.S. in International Political Economy / Business Diplomacy, then PwC (AI Technical Product Lead, ML Data Science Manager, Tech Lead at the AI & Emerging Tech Innovation Hub). Her recurring public message is that clear, structured thinking — not clever tricks — is what makes Claude useful.
persona.whats_new: Through late 2025 into 2026 her public teaching crystallized around 'clear thinking beats coding' (s3, Apr 2026) and the Prompting 101 practical guide got wide community pickup (s6, May 2026); she also contributed to Anthropic's 'Effective context engineering for AI agents' guide (s14, s17).

top_moves.fact: Publicly attributed: 'The model isn't confused. It's waiting for you to be clear.' (s3)
top_moves.inference: She values structured problem framing over prompt-hacking; a vague 'help us prompt better' ask will underwhelm her.
top_moves.move: Bring ONE concrete, structured use case from Orbt — e.g. an actual country-strategy agent prompt that's underperforming — and ask her to sharpen its structure. You'll get her real engagement.

top_moves.fact: 'AI Pilots: Product Launch, Not IT Trial' (s5); 'Don't teach too much of the basics... help them understand how to actually do their job differently, and better' (s8).
top_moves.inference: She frames adoption around changing how people work, not running a sandbox eval; she's impatient with basics.
top_moves.move: Frame Orbt's Claude adoption as a workflow transformation for your cross-border consulting delivery — show the consultant's job being done better — not as a model evaluation or 101 session.

top_moves.fact: Coined the plain-spoken 'Agents are models using tools in a loop' at the Prompting for Agents workshop (s2, s9).
top_moves.inference: She rewards concrete architecture over buzzwords; Orbt literally builds agents-in-a-loop delivered through Slack — that's her exact mental model.
top_moves.move: Walk her through Orbt's agent loop concretely (tools, the Slack delivery surface, where Claude sits in the loop). Speak in her vocabulary, not 'autonomous AI' hype.

non_obvious.insight: She's not an ML researcher — her arc is Georgetown International Political Economy / Business Diplomacy → PwC consulting & innovation hub → Anthropic Applied AI (s1, s10). Her real edge is translating between business clients and the model, i.e. deployment and prompting craft, not transformer internals. Engage her on adoption and structure, not architecture deep-dives, and you'll hit her strength.

Moderate confidence; their roles (heads-down builder vs. public-facing educator) are complementary but he may quietly probe her ML depth.

interaction_simulation.opening_30s: Open on agents in his exact-but-plain terms — 'orbt's agents are models using tools in a loop' — which lands directly on her vocabulary and signals you've read her work.
interaction_simulation.likely_arc: Quick convergence on clarity-over-tricks; she trades deployment/prompting structure, he trades architecture and the intelligence-engine reasoning — productive, fast, substance-dense.
interaction_simulation.where_it_could_stall: If it stays at education/intro level he may disengage; if he over-indexes on ML-research pedigree he undervalues her applied depth; her breadth vs. his focused rigor can talk past each other.
interaction_simulation.how_to_adapt: Bring one concrete artifact, mirror 'the model is waiting for you to be clear,' and ask her a specific context-engineering question about real agent deployments to pull the conversation to shared depth.
